other_observers: Dennis and Jim Garrison, Todd Deininger, Tom Wilberding.; Dennis and Jim Garrison probably saw the bird first as they were already at the Totten boat ramp when I pulled into the upper parking lot and saw the bird guiding across the lake.  I called Heather and Riley Morris and they saw the bird r
description: A very large dark brown bird with an unmistakable pelican bill and pouch. Crown of head was off-white that extended to back of crown and down the sides of face to just below the lower mandible. The rest of the bird was dark brown with minor shading differences. The hind neck was dark brown.  When flying in the upper wings were uniformly dark brown. The under wings were not observed, as I was above the bird and it guided down to the water surface.Behaviors: Bird was first seen guiding into a landing on Totten Lake from the northwest. It landed with the feet spread and in front of it. After landing, it rested on the surface and did not feed while I was watching it.Call: not heardPlumage: adult
photographer: Photographer: Jim Beatty, Jim Beatty, Jim Beatty
